metadata.new_reply: There's a bystander effect/general cynicism around clean energy that's very sad. someone asked me why i didn't go into environmental engineering when i believe it's so important and the honest answer, which im not proud of, is (1) it wasn't sufficiently well paid enough  (2) not enough people care that anything i did (i felt) would make a difference
metadata.diff: --- old_reply+++ new_reply@@ -1 +1 @@-People always underestimate just how leveraged energy input is in every major productivity jump. metadata.new_reply:
some questions: could the correct answer generator (which comes from being unusually insightful or analytical) be a trap in its own? because when you’re overwhelmed or ashamed it tells you to double down on analysis instead of noticing ur feelings

Something I tell people a lot is "you can’t reason someone out of something they didn’t reason their way into.”  

Reading this made me realize for the first time that that also applies to yourself, and sometimes (even often) the solution does not lie through logic at all, which is actually annoying bc the actual heuristic is far less clear and rule based
metadata.diff:
--- old_reply+++ new_reply@@ -1,2 +1,5 @@-this is so real. the “correct answer generator” is a trap because when you’re overwhelmed or ashamed, it *always* tells you to double down on analysis, instead of feeling/asking for help.
-there’s a line in a novel, “you can’t reason your way out of something you didn’t reason your way into.”  took me years to get it. toddlers are honestly the best quality control for your internal nonsense+some questions: could the correct answer generator (which comes from being unusually insightful or analytical) be a trap in its own? because when you’re overwhelmed or ashamed it tells you to double down on analysis instead of noticing ur feelings
+
+Something I tell people a lot is "you can’t reason someone out of something they didn’t reason their way into.”  
+
+Reading this made me realize for the first time that that also applies to yourself, and sometimes (even often) the solution does not lie through logic at all, which is actually annoying bc the actual heuristic is far less clear and rule based

metadata.text:

Something I tell people a lot is "you can’t reason someone out of something they didn’t reason their way into.”  

Reading this made me realize for the first time that that also applies to yourself, and sometimes (even often) the solution does not lie through logic at all, which is actually annoying bc the actual heuristic is far less clear and rule based
